title: Kiwi

overview.description: A small fruit with fuzzy brown skin and bright green flesh containing tiny edible black seeds.

method: Scoop out the flesh with a spoon, or peel away the skin and slice the fruit.
The tiny black seeds can be eaten.

learning.did-you-know: Kiwifruit were originally called Chinese gooseberries, but were renamed kiwifruit when they began to be commercially grown in New Zealand. The tiny black seeds are edible.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How a fruit can have edible skin, flesh and seeds, and how kiwifruit changes in texture as it ripens.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A visually interesting fruit that gives children the opportunity to explore edible seeds, different textures and the fact that fruit can be prepared in more than one way.
chapman-tips: A ripe kiwi should give slightly when gently pressed.
Unripe kiwis are firmer and more sour.
The skin is edible, although many people prefer to peel it because of its texture.
The tiny black seeds are edible and add a slight crunch.
Kiwifruit contains an enzyme called actinidin that can help break down proteins.
This enzyme can make fresh kiwi unsuitable for setting some gelatine desserts unless the fruit is cooked first.
notes.leftovers: Kiwifruit can be used in fruit salads, smoothies and desserts. Cut kiwi can also be frozen for smoothies.
